---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Applied AI, LLM reliability & evaluation"
description-meta: "Applied AI scientist working on information extraction, evaluation, and the reliability of LLM systems."
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    body-classes: home
---

::: {.gt-hero}
```{=html}
<svg class="gt-constellation" viewBox="0 0 1200 600" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
  <g stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" fill="none">
    <line x1="120" y1="170" x2="250" y2="90"/><line x1="120" y1="170" x2="300" y2="250"/>
    <line x1="250" y1="90" x2="450" y2="150"/><line x1="300" y1="250" x2="450" y2="150"/>
    <line x1="300" y1="250" x2="200" y2="380"/><line x1="450" y1="150" x2="600" y2="110"/>
    <line x1="450" y1="150" x2="540" y2="300"/><line x1="600" y1="110" x2="700" y2="250"/>
    <line x1="540" y1="300" x2="700" y2="250"/><line x1="600" y1="110" x2="820" y2="160"/>
    <line x1="820" y1="160" x2="700" y2="250"/><line x1="820" y1="160" x2="960" y2="120"/>
    <line x1="960" y1="120" x2="1080" y2="230"/><line x1="820" y1="160" x2="940" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="250" x2="800" y2="410"/><line x1="940" y1="320" x2="1080" y2="230"/>
    <line x1="940" y1="320" x2="800" y2="410"/><line x1="540" y1="300" x2="420" y2="430"/>
    <line x1="200" y1="380" x2="420" y2="430"/><line x1="1080" y1="230" x2="1140" y2="400"/>
  </g>
  <g fill="currentColor">
    <circle cx="120" cy="170" r="4"/><circle cx="250" cy="90" r="3"/><circle cx="300" cy="250" r="3.5"/>
    <circle cx="200" cy="380" r="3"/><circle cx="450" cy="150" r="4"/><circle cx="600" cy="110" r="3.5"/>
    <circle cx="540" cy="300" r="3"/><circle cx="700" cy="250" r="4.5"/><circle cx="960" cy="120" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="940" cy="320" r="3.5"/><circle cx="1080" cy="230" r="4"/><circle cx="800" cy="410" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="430" r="3"/><circle cx="1140" cy="400" r="3"/>
  </g>
  <circle cx="820" cy="160" r="4.5" fill="var(--fn-span-b-tag)"/>
</svg>
```

::: {.gt-hero-body}
[Yee Seng Chan]{.gt-eyebrow}

# I build [reliable LLM systems]{.gt-span data-tag="SYS"} whose behavior can be [measured]{.gt-span .gt-b data-tag="EVAL"}, inspected, and improved.

::: {.gt-links}
[GitHub](https://github.com/chanys) [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/yeesengchan) [Writing feed](writing.xml)
:::

[annotator: ys · corpus: applied nlp → llm systems]{.gt-provenance}
:::

```{=html}
<img src="ys_profile.png" class="gt-portrait" alt="Portrait of Yee Seng Chan">
```
:::

My background is a decade of NLP and information extraction, much of it deep-learning-based, judged by external evaluation, including work as a principal investigator on DARPA and IARPA programs at Raytheon BBN. The years since have been at AI startups, leading NLP and LLM work in production, most recently on LLM workflows for clinical documentation and structured extraction.

The subject is now LLMs, but the engineering question is familiar: how do you build a system whose outputs can be trusted?

## Start here

For my current positioning on LLM reliability and evaluation, read [The agent harness](posts/series/the-agent-harness/index.qmd) and [LLM evaluation, honestly](posts/series/llm-evaluation-honestly/index.qmd). These series explain how I think about the system around the model: state, tools, gates, traces, labels, judges, regression cases, and eval drift.

For the throughline from my earlier NLP work to current LLM workflows, read [Information extraction didn't disappear. It moved inside the workflow](posts/series/research-foundations-of-modern-llms/05-information-extraction/index.qmd).

The rest is grouped by argument under [Series](series.qmd). There is also a [list of projects](projects.qmd) and [more about me](about.qmd).
</content>
